tools.name: arxiv_search
tools.description: Fetch academic papers from arXiv by keyword search.

query.description: Keyword(s) for paper search, e.g. 'transformers', 'reinforcement learning', etc.

tools.name: code_agent
tools.description: Full agentic code generation pipeline. Searches real docs and Stack Overflow, generates code that incorporates what it finds, then self-reviews and iterates to fix issues before returning. Use this for any serious coding task. Much higher quality than code_assistant for complex, multi-requirement tasks.

task.description: Full description of what to build. Include: exact libraries, all constraints, quality requirements (async, typed, PR-ready, etc.). Be exhaustive — this is processed by an agentic pipeline, not a prompt.

skip_search.description: Set true to skip web research (faster, no docs context).

tools.name: code_assistant
tools.description: LLM-powered coding assistant. Use operation='generate' to write new code, 'explain' to understand existing code, 'fix' to repair bugs, 'review' to get a code review. Always wraps output in syntax-highlighted blocks.

operation.description: What to do: generate new code, explain existing code, fix bugs, or do a code review.

task.description: Description of what the code should do (required for generate/fix).

language.description: Programming language (e.g. python, javascript, rust, sql).

code.description: Existing code (required for explain, fix, review).

tools.name: crypto_fetcher
tools.description: Fetch live cryptocurrency prices and market data via CoinGecko. Use operation='search' to find a coin ID by name/symbol, or operation='price' to get the live USD price for it.

operation.description: Whether to search for a coin or get its price.

query.description: Search term or symbol if operation='search'

coin_id.description: The exact coin ID (e.g. 'bitcoin', 'ethereum') if operation='price'

tools.name: finance_fetcher
tools.description: Get stock data. Use operation='price' for current value.

tools.name: financial_calculator
tools.description: Calculate financial ratios (CAGR, PE Premium, DCF validation). Pure math, no API required.

pe_a.description: PE of asset A for pe_premium

pe_b.description: PE of base asset B for pe_premium

tools.name: google_search
tools.description: Search the web for real-time information, news, or facts. Uses DuckDuckGo as primary search engine — no API key needed. Optionally falls back to a self-hosted SearXNG instance if SEARXNG_URL is set in .env. Includes an internal LLM query optimizer (llama-3.1-8b-instant) that automatically rewrites vague or natural-language queries into precise search strings. Pass raw user intent directly. Automatically fetches and extracts the full page text of the top 3 results by default, providing deep research context instead of just short snippets.

query.description: The search query. Can be raw natural language — the internal optimizer will convert it into a precise search string.

num_results.description: Number of results to return (1–20, default 10).

fetch_webpages.description: If True, auto-fetches the full page text for the top 3 results for deeper context. Default True.

tools.name: llm_caller
tools.description: Access a Large Language Model (Llama 3.3 70B via Groq) to summarize text, answer questions, or write code.

tools.name: news_fetcher
tools.description: Fetches top N news articles with body text. Uses DuckDuckGo News as the primary source — no API key needed. Falls back to upstream google_search results if DDG fails. Bodies are fetched via requests + BeautifulSoup (no external APIs). If body fetch fails, articles are returned with snippet text instead (never errors if articles exist). Pass the original user request as raw_query for keyword optimization.

articles.description: Upstream google_search output ({{STEPS.N}}). Only used as fallback if DuckDuckGo News returns no results.

raw_query.description: Original user query. Fed into keyword optimizer sub-model.

fetch_body.description: Fetch full body text. Set False for snippet-only mode.

tools.name: sec_filings
tools.description: Fetch the latest SEC EDGAR filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K) and their URLs for a given stock ticker. Completely free.

limit.description: Maximum number of filings to return

tools.name: sub_agent
tools.description: Delegate a complex sub-task to a child Smith agent. The sub-agent has access to ALL tools (search, finance, weather, etc.) except creating more sub-agents.

task.description: Clear description of the task for the sub-agent to complete

max_depth.description: Maximum recursion depth (optional, default from config)

tools.notes: Sub-agents run a full orchestrator with all tools except sub_agent. Execution is serialized to prevent rate limits.

tools.name: technical_indicators
tools.description: Calculate stock technical indicators (RSI, MACD, SMA) and determine current trend signals.

period.description: Time period to fetch data for (needed for 200 SMA)

tools.name: tool_diagnostics
tools.description: Runs a health check on all installed tools. Detects broken imports, missing functions, or invalid metadata.

tools.name: url_reader
tools.description: Fetch and extract structured text from any web page URL. Returns the page title, content organized by headings (sections), and full text. Use after google_search to deep-read a specific result. Handles article extraction with smart fallbacks. Skips paywalled sites automatically. No API key needed.

url.description: The URL to read. Must be a valid HTTP/HTTPS URL.

max_length.description: Max content length in chars (default 10000, max 50000).

tools.name: weather_fetcher
tools.description: Get the current weather forecast (temperature, condition, wind) for any city globally.

city.description: The name of the city (e.g., 'London', 'Tokyo', 'New York').

tools.name: web_scraper
tools.description: Fetch and extract text content from any web page URL. Returns the page title and main text content.

url.description: The URL of the web page to scrape

max_length.description: Maximum length of text to return (default 5000)

tools.name: wikipedia_lookup
tools.description: Look up a topic on Wikipedia. Returns a structured summary, top-level sections with text, categories, and page URL. Useful for background context, definitions, or factual overviews to complement search/news results. No API key needed.

query.description: The topic to look up. Use proper nouns and specific terms for best results (e.g. 'Dubai', 'Free trade zone', 'Companies Act India').

tools.name: gmail
tools.description: Gmail integration: read inbox, read full email body, send emails, reply to threads, forward, star, mark read/unread, trash, and search using Gmail query syntax (e.g. 'from:boss@co.com is:unread'). Requires one-time OAuth2 browser authorization. Use message_id from read_inbox results to reference specific emails.

operation.description: One of: read_inbox, read_email, send_email, reply, forward, star, mark_read, trash, search, list_labels

message_id.description: Gmail message ID from read_inbox results
